author: Kewal Ram, Lala
title: Mazedar Natak
notes: Sindhi—Poetry serial 12 continues the annual sequence — earlier 1910 quarters carried Sindhi poetry not in our Q2/Q3 extraction range for this section. Shikarpur publisher printing at Lahore.

author: Suchehal Sain
title: Dastan Waddo
gloss: A collection of devotional verses; compiled by Mr. Hem Raj and Mian Dur Muhammad.
notes: Hindu and Muslim co-compilers (Hem Raj + Dur Muhammad) of a Sindhi devotional collection.

author: Muhammad Usman, Maulvi
title: Kimiya-i-Karamat
gloss: A book describing miracles performed by the Muhammadan saints.
notes: Same Shikarpur bookseller (Pokhar Das) behind all four Sindhi entries this page — single distribution channel for the Sindhi market via Lahore presses.

author: Panju Mal, Mr.
title: Samdarik Ilm
notes: First SINDHI—MISCELLANEOUS entry of the year (samudrika = palmistry/physiognomy, catalogued as astrology).

author: Muhammad Hasan, Sahibzada
title: Anis-ul-Muridin
gloss: In this book the author gives an account of the life of his father, Pir Abdur Rahman, for the benefit of his disciples.
notes: Dual date: 1328 Hijri / 15th September 1910. Pir-lineage hagiography for a disciple readership, 300 copies — closed devotional economy.

flags.issue: serial digit obscured by pencil X; read as 1 (no earlier PERSIAN—BIOGRAPHY entry in our 1910 extractions)

title: B. A. Course Farsi
gloss: Persian Course for the B. A. classes of the Punjab University.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Persian as university curriculum subject — the classical language now packaged as a B.A. course book.

author: Shaikh Ahmad, Hazrat
title: Maktubat-i-Imam-i-Rabbani, Hissa-i-Duwam, Daftr-i-Awwal
gloss: A collection of letters written by the author, who is a Sufi saint of great repute, to his disciples, Part II, Volume I.
copyright: M. Nur Ahmad, Amritsar; No. 173, dated the 31st October, 1910.
notes: The Maktubat of Ahmad Sirhindi (Imam-i-Rabbani, d. 1624) issued from the Mujaddidi Press — the order's founder printed by his own silsila, and copyrighted. Bracket note: Part I noticed in entry No. 6 at page 12 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending 30th September, 1909. Price range given for variant bindings.
